title: GNC Sensor Suite Design Review — 2024-12-03
text:
# GNC Sensor Suite Design Review — 2024-12-03

**Space:** K200  
**Meeting type:** Technical Design Review  
**Attendees:** Sam Dreher (GNC Lead), Dev Patel (Embedded SW), Felix Herrera (Integration), Aisha Mbeki (Test), Rowan Ellery (Electrical), Dr. Rhea Voss (CSE, remote)  
**Chair:** Sam Dreher  
**Minutes recorded by:** Dev Patel

---

## Background

During flight test campaign phase 1 (documented in DOC-467), the nav/GNC team identified that the ICM-42688-P IMU (P-1092, original) was producing attitude estimation errors of up to 2.1° in roll during high-vibration low-speed flight phases. This is above the 0.5° attitude accuracy requirement in DOC-455 (GNC ICD). The error pattern was traced to the MEMS resonance frequency of the ICM-42688-P (4.8kHz) coinciding with motor vibration harmonics from the P-1041 motors at mid-throttle settings. This issue was tracked as KES-089 (epic) and KES-091 (sub-story: IMU replacement qualification).

Additionally, GPS signal dropout events during manoeuvres were correlated with the patch antenna (P-1097) losing sky view during roll angles above 15°. This was tracked in the context of ECR-233.

## IMU Replacement Discussion

Sam Dreher presented the evaluation of the Analog Devices ADIS16505-2 tactical-grade IMU (P-1093) as a replacement. Key differentiators:

- Vibration rectification error: 0.006°/s/g² vs ICM-42688-P 0.05°/s/g² — 8x better
- Output noise: 0.008°/s/√Hz vs 0.0028°/s/√Hz for ADIS (ADIS is slightly noisier in low-vibration, but dramatically better in vibration)
- The ADIS16505-2 resonant frequency is 18kHz, well above the motor harmonic range

Dev Patel confirmed that the nav_filter.c (SRC-007) EKF can accommodate the ADIS SPI interface with a driver update, and that the attitude estimation accuracy improved to 0.3° roll in bench simulation with synthetic vibration injection.

## GPS Antenna Replacement Discussion

Rowan Ellery presented the helical antenna option (P-1098, TallysmansAU) which provides near-hemispherical coverage and maintains >20dBHz C/N₀ at 30° elevation angle, compared to the patch antenna (P-1097) which drops below 20dBHz at 45° elevation. The helical antenna also adds L5 band support which improves multi-path rejection.

## Decisions

**AGREED 1:** Replace IMU P-1092 with P-1093 (ADIS16505-2 tactical grade).
- ECR-228 to be raised by Sam Dreher (target 2024-12-10)
- SRC-007 nav_filter.c and SRC-009 gps_driver.c to be updated (Dev Patel, Q1 2025)
- DOC-455 GNC ICD to be updated (Sam Dreher, Q1 2025)
- New qualification flight test required per DOC-467 Rev B (Aisha Mbeki)

**AGREED 2:** Replace GPS antenna P-1097 with P-1098 (helical, L1/L2/L5).
- Captured in ECR-233 (same ECR as IMU, per Felix Herrera's suggestion to bundle sensor suite changes)
- DRW-534 GPS antenna installation drawing to be updated (Rowan Ellery)
- DOC-456 GPS performance spec to be updated to Rev B

## Actions

| # | Action | Owner | Due |
|---|---|---|---|
| A1 | Raise ECR-228 covering IMU and GPS antenna changes | Sam Dreher | 2024-12-10 |
| A2 | Update SRC-007 nav_filter.c for ADIS16505-2 | Dev Patel | 2025-01-31 |
| A3 | Update SRC-009 gps_driver.c for P-1098 interface | Dev Patel | 2025-01-31 |
| A4 | Update DOC-455 GNC ICD to Rev B | Sam Dreher | 2025-02-15 |
| A5 | Update DOC-456 GPS performance spec to Rev B | Rowan Ellery | 2025-02-15 |
| A6 | Plan qualification flight test series | Aisha Mbeki | 2025-01-15 |
